column1: === KELLY — Stance Coder ===
Primary role: STANCE (all 50 videos)
Secondary role: VOCAL TONE on Xinyu's daily batch (for IRR — your codes tested against Xinyu's)
Your assigned videos for SEGMENTATION: V13 V14 V15 V16 V17 V18 V19 V47
V47 (EtDS8NUYDtc) is the WORKED EXAMPLE — see the codebook walkthrough for how to segment it
[SEG] = segment boundaries already identified (but NOT split into TCUs yet — still your job to split)
STEP-BY-STEP FOR YOUR ASSIGNED VIDEOS:
1. Open transcript at the ai_mention_timestamp
2. Scroll backward to find segment_start (agenda item / topic opening)
3. Scroll forward to find segment_end (chair closing / topic shift)
4. Read transcript between boundaries → identify TCU breaks (new idea = new TCU)
5. For each TCU: fill tcu_id, tcu_transcript, tcu_start, tcu_end
6. IMMEDIATELY code stance for that TCU from transcript text only — no audio, no video
7. DELETE the original keyword-hit rows for that video once all TCUs are added
8. Post your updated file to the shared folder by end of day
FOR OTHER VIDEOS (not your assigned ones):
Wait for the assigned student to post their TCU rows.
Then open their file and fill in stance for each of their TCUs.
SECONDARY ROLE — VOCAL TONE (daily):
Each day Xinyu reports completed TCUs → you code tone on the same TCUs.
For tone: AUDIO ONLY. Look away from the screen or use audio-only playback.
Fill your tone codes in the vocal_tone column of Xinyu's file.
STANCE REMINDER: Read tcu_transcript ONLY. No video. No audio.
Neutral is NOT a catch-all — only use it when there is genuinely no evaluative content.

column7: So first, I'm going to give you a quick review of kind of a timeline and what we've done. I'm going to tell you a little bit about our FCS AI and education team that we put together, share with you work in progress, and then also our next steps. So as Mike noted, really the first step in bringing AI to kind of our end users was taken back in late 2020. So we had a really two-in-gen GBT was released and really made AI more accessible for everyone. We quickly saw that had the opportunity to be very, have an impact on our schools and our classrooms, our teachers and our students, and bring about a level of disruption potentially. So we formed a working group of teachers, ed tech coaches and instructional coaches, and with our district office staff, we really wanted to hear from our school-based personnel to hear what they were seeing in the classrooms, from their teachers, from their students, and we also wanted to have a dialogue with them from our side to be able to share what we were hearing from our vendors and the different applications that were coming on board. So two things that we immediately did before we're back to school in 2023, we added some language to our family engagement guide that provided guidance on AI use and academic integrity surrounding that. And we also provided some initial guidance to our schools, our ETCs, and our teachers on what was some appropriate uses of AI, what tools were out there. And then also the importance of never sharing personally identifiable information or any type of student data out on those websites. And then throughout that school year we did, we created a website or a webpage that our teachers had access to within class link. They could go to you to look at approved tools that went through our application kind of approval process that were safe for our teachers to use. And then last fall, we started offering some AI professional learning for our schools, from our district ed tech coaches, and some of our schools really jumped into that on their own as well and offered their own trainings. We also, within our Microsoft tenant, Microsoft 365, KOPOW was added, and that was a very important step because it created kind of a... a secure, close environment where even if something were to be input, it would be contained within our Microsoft tenant, so it would not go out into any type of large language model. And then this past January, we took that worker group and really expanded it into our AFFCS AI and education team.
